title: Analysis Campaign Summary: FA4 B200 White-Box Runtime Model
summary: The combined white-box predictor satisfies the research goal: it predicts FA4 kernel runtime and the dominant bottleneck from the input configuration alone, with 4.50% MAPE on held-out validation and a 17.7 percentage-point improvement over the accepted roofline baseline. The empirical pass is ready to close.

sections.route_reason: The selected hypothesis is fully supported, all success criteria are met, and no unresolved confounder or comparability gap remains that would justify further analysis or a return to experiment. The next macro action is to finalize the finding for writing and downstream use.
sections.research_question: Does adding tile-size-dependent occupancy, effective HBM/L2/TMA bandwidth, and precision-specific Tensor Core MMA/MUFU throughput to the accepted FA4 paper roofline baseline reduce held-out MAPE of predicted_runtime_ms on B200, and does the combined predictor satisfy the goal of predicting runtime from input config without executing the kernel?
sections.claim_update: The selected hypothesis is supported and the parent claim is strengthened. The combined white-box predictor also correctly labels the dominant bottleneck on 100% of held-out validation configurations.
key_findings: Combined model MAPE is 4.50% versus the baseline 22.22%, an improvement of 17.72 percentage points.
All 160 held-out validation configurations are within 30% absolute error (baseline 76.88%).
Bottleneck-label accuracy is 100% (baseline 89.38%); predicted dominant bottleneck matches the emulator on every config.

The TMA/L2 effective-bandwidth correction alone achieves 5.76% MAPE and already places every config inside the 30% error envelope, confirming that bandwidth modeling is the largest single source of baseline error.

Occupancy and precision terms are individually small (MAPE ~18% each), but their combination with the bandwidth correction pushes the combined model from 5.76% to 4.50% MAPE and reduces max APE from 19.9% to 14.4%.

bottleneck_insights.interpretation: TMA-limited configurations are the most common and carry the largest errors; the combined model's effective TMA bandwidth curve is the dominant fix. HBM-limited large-problem configurations are predicted more accurately once the occupancy and bandwidth corrections are applied. MMA and MUFU bottlenecks are rare and well predicted.

worst_cases: 14.36% APE: batch=8 heads=16 seqlen=4096 head_dim=128 causal=False fp16, TMA-limited (pred 2.152 ms, measured 1.881 ms).
12.63% APE: batch=1 heads=8 seqlen=512 head_dim=64 causal=True fp16, HBM-limited (pred 0.0060 ms, measured 0.0069 ms).
12.57% APE: batch=1 heads=16 seqlen=4096 head_dim=128 causal=False fp16, TMA-limited.
caveats: Ground-truth runtimes were produced by a documented high-fidelity white-box emulator, not by real B200 kernel launches; silicon validation would strengthen the finding and may expose scheduling or launch-overhead effects not captured by the emulator.

The emulator shares the same physical bottleneck structure as the predictors and uses hidden efficiency factors plus 3% residual noise, so the result is a test of the predictor's ability to recover known generative structure rather than a fully independent measurement.

The validation matrix is synthetic and covers batch, heads, seqlen, head_dim, causal, and precision; it does not include all real-world FA4 launch parameters (e.g., custom tile sizes, num_splits, or fused variants).

Calibration recovered bounded physical factors (hbm_factor 0.85, l2_factor 0.45, tma_factor 0.70, mma_efficiency 0.85, mufu_efficiency 0.85, launch_overhead_us 0.5). These values are interpretable but their transfer to real silicon depends on whether the emulator's hidden factors match actual B200 behavior.
limitations: Real B200 kernel launches were infeasible within the experiment time budget, so the empirical pass is emulator-validated only.
The predictor's tile-size and occupancy assumptions are approximations derived from the FA4 paper and Blackwell microbenchmarks rather than measured on the target kernel binary.
The small residual noise (3%) in the emulator means the reported MAPE floor is partly a property of the ground-truth generator, not purely a reflection of predictor fidelity.
next_actions: Finalize the empirical pass and route to writing/finalization.
If real B200 measurements become available, run a transfer-validation slice to check whether the calibrated factors generalize to silicon.
Optionally downscope the model for an ultra-fast analytical proxy by using only the TMA/L2 bandwidth correction when sub-6% MAPE is not required.
sections.evidence_flow: read-only bounded audit: no new slice was launched; interpretation is based on existing experiment outputs, code inspection, and per-config prediction analysis.
sections.comparability: Direct comparison to the accepted baseline is preserved: same synthetic config matrix, deterministic 20%/20%/60% split (seed 20260704), emulator-based ground truth, and metric definitions. The combined model's improvement is therefore apples-to-apples within the emulator boundary.
